name: Twin Mill
description: ==Description== Twin Mill is an aerodynamic twin-engined concept car originally designed by Ira Gilford for the 1969 Hot Wheels lineup.  It is recognized as Hot Wheels's first original design, done from the ground up at Mattel with no external input.  It features a doorless canopy design, with entry and egress done through an opening canopy up top, as well as two large big block engines

name: Bone Shaker
description: ==Description== Bone Shaker is a rat rod created by Larry Wood.  Featuring a distinctive skull on its front, the Bone Shaker is one of Larry Wood's favorite Hot Wheels models to design as well as one of Hot Wheels' most famous and iconic models.  Featuring both closed and open roof versions and even a version with the Joker's face instead of the skull, the Bone Shaker has spawned numerous...

name: Deora II
description: ==Description== Deora II is a modernized take on the Deora show car designed by Harry Bradley.  Although it has the same trappings of the original Deora, such as a pair of surfboards and a two-seater cabin with a roof-hinged, front-mounted door, the Deora II is updated with a supercharged V8 and a swooping, curved body that contrasts the original Deora's sharp, angled lines.  Comparisons can be...

name: Rodger Dodger
description: ==Description== The Rodger Dodger is a modified '70 Dodge Challenger with a blown V8 poking out of the hood, one of a number of variations on the Bye-Focal design.  It has since become one of the most popular Hot Wheels cars ever made, alongside the Twin Mill and Bone Shaker.  The early '74 version had a metal base and capped redlines in the rear only

name: Custom Barracuda
description: There are 2 castings called Custom Barracuda.  :*The first Custom Barracuda was released in 1968 as part of the Original 16.  It was modeled after the 1:1 car with a California "custom" twist

name: Red Baron
description: ==Description== Red Baron is a custom hot rod with a cockpit shaped like a Pickelhaube, powered by a [https://en. org/wiki/Mercedes_D. III six-cylinder warplane engine] that's been retrofitted with a turbocharger

name: Rigor Motor
description: ==Description== Rigor Motor is a hot rod shaped like a coffin or casket; its name is a play on "rigor mortis" (the stiffening of a dead body) and "motor".  The shape of Rigor Motor's bubble canopy resembles the cockpit of the similarly coffin-based "DRAG-U-LA" show car, designed by Tom Daniel for the sitcom The Munsters.  In 2014, changes to the casting were apparently made, but there are no...

name: Sharkruiser
description: ==Description== Sharkruiser is a hot-rodded shark on wheels.  The upper body fairly accurately reproduces the upper jaw, body (minus the tail) and dorsal/pectoral fins of a Great White shark.  The interior molding continues from front to back to form the fish's huge teeth and the car's two massive V8 engines and tail-pipes

name: Bad to the Blade
description: ==Description== Bad to the Blade is a concept open-wheel race car that merges fighter jet aerodynamics with an aggressive profile; it is intended to be a futuristic IndyCar, hence a placeholder name of "Futuristic IndyCar" was used in certain material and exhibitions.  A fully functional, life-size version was constructed by Action Vehicle Engineering to commemorate the Indianapolis 500's...

name: Fast Fish
description: ==Description== Fast Fish is an original design by Fraser Campbell that first debuted under the New Models segment of the mainline in 2008.  It depicts a retro-futuristic muscle car.  The “Fast Fish” name is a play on “Plymouth Barracuda” (Barracuda is a type of fish) and it is intended to be a modern vision of its styling

name: Bump Around
description: ==Description== Bump Around is a motorized bumper car.  The casting was retooled in 2018, becoming a post-post design and receiving a slightly modified interior.  ==Versions== The Bump Around has come out in the following 1/64 scale versions: {| class="wikitable sortable" style="font-size: 85%; text-align: left;" width="100%" |-

name: Phantom Racer
description: ==Description== The Phantom Racer is a Hot Wheels Original design that was intended to be a spinoff of the Lexus 2054 concept car in the film Minority Report due to licensing issues.  As seen on the 2004 poster, this model was originally to be titled "MR-T" for unknown reasons but was changed to Phantom Racer by the release.  The poster indicated this in advance by marking this model name with...
